instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: James, a slave of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, to the twelve tribes in the dispersion: Rejoice!
output: Jacobo, esclavo de Dios y del Señor Jesucristo, a las doce tribus que están en la dispersión: ¡Regocijaos!

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Count it all joy, my brothers, whenever you fall into various trials,
output: Hermanos míos, tened por sumo gozo cuando os halléis en diversas pruebas,

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Knowing that the proving of your faith works out endurance.
output: sabiendo que la prueba de vuestra fe produce perseverancia.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And let endurance have its perfect work that you may be perfect and entire, lacking in nothing.
output: Mas tenga la perseverancia su obra perfecta, para que seáis perfectos y cabales, sin que os falte cosa alguna.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: But if any one of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and does not reproach, and it will be given to him.
output: Y si alguno de vosotros tiene falta de sabiduría, pídala a Dios, el cual da a todos con liberalidad y sin reproche, y le será dada.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: But let him ask in faith, doubting nothing, for he who doubts is like the surge of the sea, driven by the wind and tossed about.
output: Pero pida con fe, no dudando nada; porque el que duda es semejante al oleaje del mar, que es arrastrado por el viento y echado de una parte a otra.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: For that man must not suppose that he will receive anything from the Lord;
output: No piense, pues, ese hombre, que recibirá cosa alguna del Señor,

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He is a double-souled man, unstable in all his ways.
output: siendo hombre de doble ánimo, inestable en todos sus caminos.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And let the lowly brother boast in his exaltation,
output: El hermano de humilde condición, gloríese en su exaltación;

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And the rich in his being brought low, because like a flower of the grass he will pass away.
output: y el rico, en su humillación; porque él pasará como la flor de la hierba.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: For the sun rises with its scorching heat and withers the grass, and its flower falls off, and the beauty of its appearance is destroyed; so also the rich man will fade away in his pursuits.
output: Porque el sol se levanta con calor abrasador y seca la hierba, y su flor se cae, y la hermosura de su apariencia perece; así también se marchitará el rico en todas sus empresas.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Blessed is the man who endures trial, because when he has become approved by testing, he will receive the crown of life, which He promised to those who love Him.
output: Bienaventurado el varón que soporta la prueba; porque una vez aprobado, recibirá la corona de vida, que el Señor ha prometido a los que le aman.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Let no one say when he is tempted, I am tempted of God; for God cannot be tempted of evil, and He Himself tempts no one.
output: Cuando alguno es tentado, no diga que es tentado de parte de Dios; porque Dios no puede ser tentado por el mal, ni Él tienta a nadie;

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: But each one is tempted when he is drawn away and enticed by his own lusts;
output: sino que cada uno es tentado cuando es atraído y seducido por su propia concupiscencia.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Then the lust, having conceived, gives birth to sin; and the sin, when it is fully grown, brings forth death.
output: Entonces la concupiscencia, después que ha concebido, da a luz el pecado; y el pecado, siendo consumado, engendra la muerte.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Do not be deceived, my beloved brothers.
output: Amados hermanos míos, no os engañéis.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: All good giving and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom is no variation or shadow cast by turning.
output: Toda buena dádiva y todo don perfecto viene de lo alto, y desciende del Padre de las luces, en el cual no hay mudanza, ni oscurecimiento causado por rotación.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: He brought us forth by the word of truth, purposing that we might be a kind of firstfruits of His creatures.
output: Él, de Su voluntad, nos engendró por la palabra de verdad, para que seamos en cierto modo primicias de Sus criaturas.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: You know this, my beloved brothers; but let every man be quick to hear, slow to speak, slow to wrath;
output: Sabéis esto, mis amados hermanos. Pero que cada uno sea pronto para oír, tardo para hablar, tardo para airarse;

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: For the wrath of man does not accomplish the righteousness of God.
output: porque la ira del hombre no cumple la justicia de Dios.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Therefore putting away all filthiness and the abundance of malice, receive in meekness the implanted word, which is able to save your souls.
output: Por lo cual, desechando toda inmundicia y abundancia de malicia, recibid con mansedumbre la palabra implantada, la cual puede salvar vuestras almas.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: And become doers of the word and not hearers only, who delude themselves.
output: Y sed hacedores de la palabra, no tan solamente oidores, engañándoos a vosotros mismos.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: Because if anyone is a hearer of the word and not a doer, this one is like a man considering in a mirror the face he was born with;
output: Porque si alguno es oidor de la palabra pero no hacedor de ella, éste es semejante al hombre que considera en un espejo el rostro con el cual nació.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: For he considers himself and goes away, and immediately forgets what kind of person he is.
output: Porque él se considera a sí mismo, y se va, y luego olvida qué clase de persona es.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: But he who looks into the perfect law, the law of freedom, and continues in it, becoming not a forgetful hearer but a doer of the work, this one will be blessed in his doing.
output: Mas el que mira atentamente en la perfecta ley, la de la libertad, y persevera en ella, no siendo oidor olvidadizo, sino hacedor de la obra, éste será bienaventurado en lo que hace.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: If anyone thinks himself to be religious and yet does not bridle his tongue but deceives his heart, this one’s religion is vain.
output: Si alguno se cree religioso, y no refrena su lengua, sino que engaña su corazón, la religión del tal es vana.

instruction: Translate the following verse into Spanish in a strictly literal and faithful manner, preserving structure and terminology.
input: This is pure and undefiled religion before our God and Father: to visit orphans and widows in their affliction and to keep oneself unspotted from the world.
output: La religión pura e incontaminada delante de nuestro Dios y Padre es ésta: visitar a los huérfanos y a las viudas en sus tribulaciones, y guardarse sin mancha del mundo.
